title: Year 6 New Zealand Curriculum - Ministry of Education
content:
## Year 6 New Zealand Curriculum
Information to help you understand what your child will have learned by the end of their sixth year at school, and ideas for activities to help them with their learning at home.
- Guide for the New Zealand Curriculum – Years 0 to 8
- First 6 months at school

- Year 2 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 3 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 4 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 5 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 6 New Zealand Curriculum English in Year 6 Reading books at home in Year 6 Mathematics and Statistics in Year 6 Health and Physical Education in Year 6 Science in Year 6 Technology in Year 6 Social Sciences in Year 6 The Arts in Year 6

We’re inviting parents and caregivers to share their feedback on the Parent Portal to help us improve the information and support we provide. Take a few minutes to complete our short survey.
Parent Portal survey – Survey Monkey

## By the end of Year 6 #
Knowing what your child is learning in their first full year of school will help you support and guide them as they begin learning reading, writing and maths every day.
- English in Year 6 For English in Year 6, your child’s teacher will focus on oral language, reading, and writing. Find out more
For English in Year 6, your child’s teacher will focus on oral language, reading, and writing.
- Reading books at home in Year 6 Find out about supporting your child’s reading in the sixth year of school. Find out more

### Reading books at home in Year 6
Find out about supporting your child’s reading in the sixth year of school.
- Mathematics and Statistics in Year 6 In Years 4, 5 and 6 (phase 2), the focus at school is on helping children expand their knowledge and work together with others. Find out more

### Mathematics and Statistics in Year 6
In Years 4, 5 and 6 (phase 2), the focus at school is on helping children expand their knowledge and work together with others.
- Health and Physical Education in Year 6 In Year 6, your child will learn about puberty and self-care, communication skills, choreographed movements and sports techniques. Find out more

### Health and Physical Education in Year 6
In Year 6, your child will learn about puberty and self-care, communication skills, choreographed movements and sports techniques.
- Science in Year 6 In Year 6, your child will begin to evaluate simple data, describe patterns, trends and ideas, question information, and ground explanations in scientific thinking. Find out more
In Year 6, your child will begin to evaluate simple data, describe patterns, trends and ideas, question information, and ground explanations in scientific thinking.
- Technology in Year 6 In Year 6, students use a range of materials and techniques to design solutions for real-world problems, thinking logically and refining ideas through testing and feedback. Find out more

### Technology in Year 6
In Year 6, students use a range of materials and techniques to design solutions for real-world problems, thinking logically and refining ideas through testing and feedback.
- Social Sciences in Year 6 In Year 6, your child will start to connect important concepts such as migration and democracy, learning about history, citizenship rights and responsibilities, business and enterprise. Find out more

### Social Sciences in Year 6
In Year 6, your child will start to connect important concepts such as migration and democracy, learning about history, citizenship rights and responsibilities, business and enterprise.
- The Arts in Year 6 In Year 6, your child will apply creative techniques, make thoughtful artistic choices, and explore how culture and history shape art forms. Find out more
In Year 6, your child will apply creative techniques, make thoughtful artistic choices, and explore how culture and history shape art forms.
